license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.hhwb37e
title: Euplotes focardii RNA extraction

steps_raw.instruction: Filter and collect Euplotes focardii cells (ca.40000 cells) by centrifuging at 4°C for 5 min at 3000 rpm.
Dissolve the cell pellet in 750 µl Trizol reagent solution (3 volumes of Trizol to 1 volume of cells), vortex for 2 min.
Incubate 5 min at room temperature, add 200 µl of chloroform to the solution and vortex for 15 sec.
Incubate 15 min at room temperature then centrifuge the sample at 4°C for 20 min at 12000 rpm.
Move the supernatant containing RNA in a new tube and precipitate the RNA by adding isopropanol vol1:1.
Incubate 10 min at room temperature, collect the RNA by centrifugation at 4°C for 30 min at 13000 rpm and discard the supernatant.
Wash twice the RNA pellet with 1 ml of ethanol 75% (in DPEC water) by centrifuging 5 min at 8000 rpm and then let the pellet dry.
Resuspend the RNA pellet in 20-30 µl of DPEC water and incubate 10 min at 55-60°C.
Treat the RNA sample with DNAse I to remove genomic DNA.
Treat the RNA sample with DNAse I to remove genomic DNA.
